topic.what: Detecting unauthorized incorporation of retrieved document context in RL fine-tuning pipelines through behavioral canaries
topic.how: Instrument preference data with trigger-conditioned stylistic signals that induce measurable policy shifts if document context influences RL training
topic.why_matters: Current auditing methods (membership inference, canary-based) rely on memorization which is ineffective for RLFT where influence manifests as behavioral distribution shifts rather than verbatim reproduction

characteristics.what_it_measures: AUROC for distinguishing trained-with vs trained-without document context
characteristics.evidence: Table showing AUROC across datasets models and RL algorithms
characteristics.context: Controlled experiments at 1% effective injection rate

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of incorrectly flagging compliant systems as violating
characteristics.evidence: Operating point for detection rate of 67%

characteristics.what_it_measures: True positive rate for document-context incorporation at FPR=10%
characteristics.evidence: Main results across controlled RLFT experiments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effective canary injection rate in post-curation training data
characteristics.context: Practically attainable for external auditor with programmatic access

characteristics.what_it_measures: RL transmission of reward signals as behavioral influence
characteristics.vs_baseline: RL attenuates reward-model biases rather than amplifying
characteristics.evidence: Maintains sufficient signal to produce measurable trigger-conditioned shifts
characteristics.context: RLFT behaves as lossy transmission process

characteristics.what_itasures: Downstream task performance after canary injection
characteristics.evidence: Experiments across multiple datasets base models and RL algorithms
characteristics.context: Detection achievable without degrading task utility

good_for: auditing RLFT pipelines for document-context incorporation
detecting behavioral influence vs verbatim memorization
gray-box auditors with token-level log probability access
not_for: white-box auditing with training data access
detecting specific document inclusion rather than category-level incorporation
requires: gray-box access to token-level log probabilities
programmatic interaction capability with LLM service
ability to inject trigger-conditioned preference data

novelty: Formulates RL-stage data provenance as behavioral distinguishability problem under reward-mediated optimization
Introduces behavioral canaries as end-to-end mechanism probing document-context influence in RLFT
Establishes feasibility baseline showing RL-transmitted signals are detectable at 1% inclusion
Demonstrates RL behaves as lossy transmission where training signals are suppressed but not eliminated
open_problems: deployment-realistic audits with realistic curator filtering
defense mechanisms against behavioral canary auditing
threshold optimization for practical deployment auditing
